hero.heading: 📊 Grafana - Visualize Your Metrics
hero.subtitle: Build rich dashboards, explore telemetry, and get answers from your metrics and traces.
hero.whyLearn: Grafana is the standard visualization layer in cloud-native stacks. It's used to turn raw telemetry into actionable insights.

storytelling.problem: You have a running service but you can't tell why latency spikes or which component is failing. Logs are noisy and you need a quick signal.
storytelling.solution: Grafana connects to metrics, traces, and logs backends and provides dashboards, alerts, and exploration tools so you can quickly find patterns and root causes.
storytelling.analogy: Grafana is like the cockpit of a plane—pilots don't inspect each mechanical part when something goes wrong; they read the instruments to understand what's happening immediately.
storytelling.realWorldExample: Teams use Grafana to correlate CPU, request latency, and error rates on a single dashboard to identify the microservice causing an outage within minutes.
storytelling.deepDive: Grafana supports many data sources (Prometheus, Loki, Elasticsearch, Graphite, InfluxDB, Jaeger). Dashboards are composed of panels with queries and visualizations. Features include templating, annotations, alerting, and Loki for logs.

architectureFlow.title: Connect Data Sources
architectureFlow.description: Add Prometheus, Loki, Elasticsearch, or cloud metrics as data sources
architectureFlow.details: Grafana queries backend stores on demand; panels use queries to render charts and tables.

architectureFlow.title: Create Dashboards
architectureFlow.description: Compose panels displaying metrics, logs, and traces
architectureFlow.details: Use queries, transformations, and variables to build reusable dashboards for SLOs, performance, and reliability.

architectureFlow.title: Set Alerts
architectureFlow.description: Turn thresholds and query results into alerts
architectureFlow.details: Grafana Alerting evaluates rules and integrates with notification channels (Slack, PagerDuty, email).

architectureFlow.title: Share & Collaborate
architectureFlow.description: Publish dashboards and create snapshots for stakeholders
architectureFlow.details: Dashboards can be templated and shared across teams; RBAC controls access.

beginner.description: Connect Prometheus or your cloud provider metrics to Grafana.
Create a simple dashboard with a graph and a singlestat panel.
Parameterize dashboards to view metrics across services or environments.
Inspect queries and datasource responses to debug panel data.

intermediate.description: Manage dashboards with JSON or provisioning to keep them in Git.
Write alert rules and connect notification channels.
Transform query results in Grafana UI to shape data for visualization.

advanced.description: Use or build community plugins for custom visualizations.
Run Grafana behind a load balancer with a durable backend for sessions and data sources.

intro.what: Grafana is an open-source platform for analytics and monitoring that lets you query, visualize, alert on, and explore your metrics and logs.
intro.why: Visualizing telemetry helps teams spot trends, regressions, and root causes quickly. Grafana unifies multiple backends with a single dashboarding UX.
intro.industry_importance: Grafana is widely adopted across startups and enterprises as the standard dashboarding layer for observability.

beginnerMistakes.title: Too Many Panels
beginnerMistakes.description: Overloading dashboards makes them slow and hard to read.
beginnerMistakes.howToFix: Create focused dashboards per SLO and use templating/variables.

productionSection.monitoring: Use Grafana with Prometheus for metrics and Loki for logs; back up dashboards; use RBAC for team access.

labs.title: Build a Service Dashboard
labs.description: Create a dashboard for a sample microservice using Prometheus metrics

steps.title: Connect Prometheus
steps.instruction: Add Prometheus data source to Grafana

steps.title: Add Panels
steps.instruction: Create graphs for request rate, error rate, and latency

labs.expectedOutput: Dashboard shows live metrics for the sample service

interviewQuestions.question: What is Grafana used for?
interviewQuestions.answer: Visualizing and alerting on metrics, logs, and traces from multiple backends.

dashboard_shortcuts.description: Create a new dashboard from the Grafana UI
Inspect query results and backend responses
Configure where alerts should be sent

usage_tips: Use templating variables to switch between environments and services
Keep dashboards focused on one SLO or one service area
Pair Grafana with Prometheus for metrics and Loki for logs
